title: Kalyanamthan Kattikittu Lyrics Saamy
stanzas: <div>Kalyanamthan Kattikittu Song Lyrics Saamy

Kalyanamthaan kattikittu
oadi polamaa
illai oadipoyi kalyanamthaan
kattikalamaa

thaliyathaan kattikittu
peththukalamaa
illa pulla kutti peththukittu
kattikalaama

saamburani vaasathula
valarntha sirukki ne
ada sagunam parthu
sadangu paarthu
siricha kirikki nee

saamikellaam sagunam
yaedhu thaevaiyillaiye
nee saaku poakku
solvathellaam niyaayam illaiye

kattikalamaa

naan unna kaeten
kaaranjaaramaa
unaku samikka theriyumaa

yaen iduppu oaramaa

manja poosi kulichita

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kalyanamthan Kattikittu?

The song celebrates the joy and excitement of marriage, capturing the festive spirit of weddings.

Who are the singers of this song?

KK and Srilekha Parthasarathy lend their voices to this energetic track.

Who composed the music for Saamy?

The music for Saamy was composed by Harrish Jeyaraj.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

The song is set in a celebratory context, highlighting the joyous moments of a wedding.

When was the song released?

The song was released in the year 2003.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Kalyanamthan Kattikittu is a lively and energetic song from the movie Saamy, capturing the joyous essence of a wedding celebration. Sung by KK and Srilekha Parthasarathy, the track is filled with vibrant beats and playful lyrics that convey the excitement of marriage. The song is set against a backdrop of festivities, making it a perfect choice for weddings and parties. Harrish Jeyaraj's composition adds a dynamic rhythm that enhances the celebratory mood, making it a timeless favorite in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Kalyanamthan Kattikittu from the movie Saamy is a vibrant celebration of love and marriage. With Harrish Jeyaraj's catchy tunes and the dynamic vocals of KK and Srilekha Parthasarathy, this song captures the joy and excitement of tying the knot. Dive into the festive beats and lively lyrics that make this track a timeless favorite for weddings and celebrations.
stanzaMeanings: Shall we get married and run away, or run away and then get married?
Shall we tie the knot and have a child, or have a child and then get married?
You grew up in the smell of the kitchen, you laugh at traditions and customs.
For the gods, there's no need for omens. What you say is not fair.
Shall we get married and run away, or run away and then get married?
Shall we tie the knot and have a child, or have a child and then get married?
Do you cook delicious food and talk sweetly?
Will you serve rice with ghee and lentils?
I asked you for healthy food. Do you know how to cook?
I will make spicy chili curry. You taste it and see.
Do you know how to cook crab, fry chicken, and make soup with mutton?
Why is my waist on one side? Can you fix it?
Shall we get married and run away, or run away and then get married?
Shall we tie the knot and have a child, or have a child and then get married?
You show off like a village hero with a mustache.
You are scared like a chicken crossing the road.
I will do as you wish, no limits.
When the month of Panguni comes, I will give you a gift. Adjust a little until then.
When I see you, my heart races, and my body shivers.
Shall I grind the lentils and make a dish for you all night?
Shall we get married and run away, or run away and then get married?
Shall we tie the knot and have a child, or have a child and then get married?
You grew up in the smell of the kitchen, you laugh at traditions and customs.
For the gods, there's no need for omens. What you say is not fair.
Shall we tie the knot and have one child, or have many children and then get married?
